articles.title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence models are multiplying fast, and competition is stiff. With so many players crowding the space, which one will be the best — and who decides that? Arena, formerly LM Arena, has emerged as the de facto public leaderboard for frontier LLMs, influencing funding, launches, and PR cycles. In just seven months, the startup went from a UC Berkeley PhD research […]

articles.title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
articles.summary: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

articles.title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
articles.summary: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.

articles.title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid
articles.summary: Patreon CEO Jack Conte says AI companies should pay creators for training data, arguing their fair use defense falls apart when they license content from major publishers.

The Download: The Pentagon’s new AI plans, and next-gen nuclear reactors
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says  The Pentagon plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their…
